ocr.text: This report presents a detailed analysis of genomic sequencing data derived from various animal subjects, as outlined in the
accompanying table. The focus of the study centers on the comparison of sequencing metrics across different templates,
specifically examining the performance of the Itga5b, Tmem8, and Ankrd28 genes. Notably, the "Number of total reads" and

the "Number of mapped reads" serve as critical indicators of sequencing efficiency and data quality. 

For instance, the data
reveals a total of 766,207,530 reads, with an impressive 720,413,573 mapped reads, resulting in a mapping efficiency of 94.0%.
This high percentage underscores the reliability of the sequencing process and the integrity of the data obtained.
Animal ID  Number of total reads  Number of mapped reads  Number of bases in mapped reads  Mean depth  Genome coverage
Template  Itga5b 3'  Itga5b WT  Tmem8 3'  Tmem8 WT  Ankrd28 3'  Ankrd28 WT
Furthermore, the "Mean depth" of 27.3 X indicates robust coverage across the genome, complemented by a total of 72.8 Gb of
bases in mapped reads. Such metrics are essential for ensuring comprehensive genome coverage and facilitating further analysis
of genetic variations within the specified templates. This foundational data supports ongoing research into the functional
implications of the genes studied, contributing to a deeper understanding of their roles in biological processes.
